ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT DESOTO CAB COMPANY, INC'S MOTION TO DISMISS AND FOR SANCTIONS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DESOTO CAB COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS AND FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT PLAINTIFF KAMAILEIMILA DEL FERRO HAS FAILED TO PAY A MONETARY SANCTIONS ORDER, THAT FAILURE IS NOT A BASIS TO DISMISS THIS CASE IN THE FACE OF CLEAR EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WISHES TO PROCEED WITH THE CASE. THE REMEDY FOR AN UNPAID MONETARY SANCTIONS ORDER IS TO CONVERT IT INTO A JUDGMENT OR USE IT AS A CREDIT AGAINST ANY MONIES AWARDED TO THE SANCTIONED PARTY AT THE CONCLUSION OF THE CASE. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Defendant Desoto Cab Company, Inc.'S Motion To Dismiss; And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Desoto Cab Company, Inc.'s Motion To Dismiss; And For Sanctions.

Defendant Desoto Cab Company, Inc.'s motion to dismiss and for sanctions is denied in its entirety. Assuming without deciding that plaintiff Kamaileimila Del Ferro has failed to pay a monetary sanctions order, that failure is not a basis to dismiss this case in the face of clear evidence that plaintiff wishes to proceed with the case. The remedy for an unpaid monetary sanctions order is to convert it into a judgment or use it as a credit against any monies awarded to the sanctioned party at the conclusion of the case.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, Maria.Torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
